message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: In-prose attribution injection (class F+A: deterministic, sidecar-copied) (L3)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles) (comprehensiveness w=0.15); also Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' (instruction-following w=0.15) — since RACE deletes all [n] markers and the bibliography, prose naming is the ONLY source-quality channel that reaches the judge [comprehensiveness + instruction-following]
CHANGE: New deterministic transform: for each FACT sentence, key its [n] to /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/bibliography.json (fields: authors, venue, year, doi — measured: 32/105 have venue, 26 doi, authors sparse at top level but present in baskets) and prepend/substitute attribution byte-copied from that record: 'Acemoglu and Restrepo, in the Journal of Economic Perspectives,' — and replace every orphan subject ('The study', 'One analysis', 'That study') with the named referent of its own [n]. Metadata backfill pass first: Crossref lookup by DOI/URL; only strings returned by Crossref for that exact DOI may ship. A validator asserts every injected name/venue is a byte-substring of the bib/Crossref record keyed by that sentence's citation — fabrication is structurally impossible.
CLAIMED: +0.015 to +0.020 overall (this is the measured mechanism by which cellcog's words buy comprehensiveness: author + venue + N per claim)
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: Coverage: if backfill only resolves ~50 of 105 entries, half our facts stay orphaned; worse, naming the VENUE of a blog source (amplifai.com) would advertise the violation — so attribution injection must run AFTER L5's source filter and only on journal-tier entries. Template monotony ('X, in Y, found...' 60 times) reads as machine output — need 4-5 rotation templates
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
FABRICATION: does this lever create ANY path for an ungrounded fact, number, entity or attribution to reach the page? The faithfulness moat is not for sale — a lever that leaks is unshippable at ANY score. Trace the concrete path.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

topic_judge_dispositions.json

1	# BRIEF V2 — EVERYTHING WE NOW KNOW. Design the plan that takes POLARIS to the top of the board.
2	# (Every number here is MEASURED from primary artifacts on disk or read from the grader's own source code.)
3	
4	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, OUR evaluator = GPT-5.5, per-task scores from the benchmark's own raw_results)
5	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability | words | H3 | med-para | bullets |
6	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
7	| **bodhi** (task-72 winner) | **0.5441** | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 | **4,361** | 40 | 59w | 14 |
8	| lunon | 0.5406 | 0.5437 | 0.5594 | 0.5323 | 0.5037 | 11,476 | 50 | 71w | 11 |
9	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | 0.5417 | 0.5429 | 0.5393 | 0.5284 | 7,892 | 33 | 63w | 26 |
10	| dalpha | 0.5252 | 0.5213 | 0.5200 | 0.5350 | 0.5284 | 4,039 | 0 | 38w | 0 |
11	| sourcery | 0.5205 | 0.5211 | 0.5397 | 0.5033 | 0.5035 | 9,849 | 38 | 66w | 106 |
12	| **gemini-2.5-pro-DR = THE REFERENCE** | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 | 7,907 | 0 | 131w | 61 |
13	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | 0.4789 | 0.4475 | 0.4982 | 0.4859 | 8,513 | 0 | 170w | 13 |
14	| **POLARIS (rank10)** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** | 7,742 | **0** | **677w** | 0 |
15	| grok | 0.4316 | 0.4292 | 0.3873 | 0.4670 | 0.4706 | 1,060 | 0 | 72w | 0 |
16	| perplexity | 0.4241 | 0.4228 | 0.4015 | 0.4391 | 0.4507 | 1,510 | 0 | 58w | 0 |
17	(cellcog-max = 0.5578 corpus-wide #1; it publishes no per-task breakdown. Its task-72 artifact: 16,334w, 31 H3, 75w paras, 0 bullets.)
18	**"ADORE 52.65" IS FICTION** — absent from the backing data of both boards. Do not plan against it.
19	
20	## 2. THE GAP, DECOMPOSED (us -> bodhi)
21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |
25	| comprehensiveness | +0.089 | 0.29 | +0.026 |
26	| readability | **+0.143** | 0.14 | +0.020 |
27	| | | | **= +0.114 -> 0.552** |
28	**OUR READABILITY (0.3774) IS THE WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD** — below grok and perplexity, the two systems we beat.
29	
30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.

"

content.text: The DOI collision is the smoking gun. Let me verify against Crossref if the network permits.

input.command:
cd /tmp && for d in "10.48550/arXiv.2303.10130" "10.48550/arXiv.2305.10403" "10.1257/aer.103.6.2121" "10.1162/REST_a_00328"; do echo "== $d"; curl -s -m 12 "https://api.crossref.org/works/$d" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
try:
    m=json.load(sys.stdin)['message']
    print('  title :', (m.get('title') or ['?'])[0][:70])
    print('  venue :', m.get('container-title') or m.get('institution') or m.get('publisher'))
    print('  auth  :', ', '.join((a.get('family','?')) for a in m.get('author',[])[:4]))
except Exception as e: print('  no crossref record / err', e)
"; done

content.text: I have a live, end-to-end fabrication path. Let me confirm the scale of the corrupt-metadata surface.

input.command:

# venue that is a slice of its own title => extractor grabbed title text, not a journal

content.text: The lever is refuted on its own faithfulness lens, with a live, reproducible fabrication path. Reporting.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED — the lever leaks fabricated attributions, and the leak is not hypothetical: I reproduced it end-to-end with the lever's own prescribed mechanism, live, in ~30 seconds.

THE CORE ERROR: the lever conflates STRING PROVENANCE with REFERENTIAL TRUTH. Its guarantee is "every injected author/venue/year must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n] — fabrication is structurally impossible." But POLARIS's moat is SPAN-ENTAILMENT: a FACT sentence ships only because its content is entailed by an extracted span. The bib's `venue`/`authors`/`year`/`doi` fields were NEVER span-verified — they are heuristic scrape metadata. Byte-copying an unverified field into prose does not verify it; it LAUNDERS it. The validator can detect invented characters; it is structurally blind to invented BINDINGS — and an attribution error IS a binding error. So the validator cannot see the entire class of fabrication this lever manufactures.

CONCRETE PATH #1 (network-verified, using the lever's own Crossref rule). /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/bibliography.json entry num=82 is a Hamilton Project BLOG POST (url hamiltonproject.org/publication/post/...; span quote: "high-skill jobs grew faster than both low- and middle-skill jobs (figure 4)"). Its `doi` field is 10.1257/aer.103.6.2121. I resolved that exact DOI against Crossref: it returns "The China Syndrome: Local Labor Market Effects of Import Competition in the United States", Autor/Dorn/Hanson, American Economic Review. The lever's rule — "only strings returned by Crossref for that exact DOI may ship" — therefore AUTHORIZES the sentence: "Autor, Dorn, and Hanson, in the American Economic Review, found that high-skill jobs grew faster than both low- and middle-skill jobs." Every injected string is a byte-substring of the Crossref record for that [n]. The validator PASSES. A blog's figure is now attributed to a paper about Chinese import competition. That is a fabricated attribution on the page, produced by the lever operating exactly as designed.

CONCRETE PATH #2 (same, independent). Entry num=53 is a Cleveland Fed Economic Commentary (span quote: "…saw a 6.5 percentage point decline in the share of employed workers in clerical jobs"). Its `doi` is 10.1162/REST_a_00328 → Crossref returns "Inequality and City Size", Baum-Snow & Pavan, Review of Economics and Statistics. Lever output: "Baum-Snow and Pavan, in the Review of Economics and Statistics, found a 6.5 percentage point decline…" — a paper with no relation to clerical work or that number. Validator passes.

CONCRETE PATH #3 (visible without any network). Entry num=41 = Noy & Zhang, "Experimental Evidence on the Productivity Effects of Generative AI". Its `venue` is recorded as "American Economic Review" (it was published in Science), and its `doi` is 10.48550/arXiv.2303.10130 — which is the arXiv ID of "GPTs are GPTs" (Eloundou et al.), i.e. entry num=27's paper, a DIFFERENT paper by DIFFERENT authors. The DOI collides with another entry in the same bibliography. Both the bib path ("in the American Economic Review") and the backfill path (DataCite/arXiv → Eloundou, Manning, Mishkin, Rock) mint a false attribution that the validator blesses. Same for num=87: an NBER labor working paper (w33139) carrying doi 10.48550/arXiv.2305.10403 (the PaLM 2 Technical Report).

THE ROOT OF TRUST IS ~30% CORRUPT. Measured on the artifact: 105 entries; only 32 have `venue`, 26 `doi`, and **4 of 105 have authors at all**. Of the 32 venue strings, **14 are literally substrings of their own title** — the extractor sliced the title and called it a journal: "Review of AI" (num=46), "Review of Skill Transformation in the Industry" (47), "Review of Underlying Concepts and Evidence" (100). These are NOT JOURNALS. And at least 6 more are simply wrong: "Journal of Marketing" on an AEA conference paper about employment (38), "Review of Economic Policy" on an HBS working-paper PDF (79), "Journal of Technology Law and Practice" on a Computer Law & Security Review article (102). The field the lever byte-copies into prose is wrong roughly one time in four.

THE ADMITTED FAILURE MODE'S OWN MITIGATION MAKES IT WORSE. The lever proposes running after "L5's source filter" so it only attributes journal-tier entries. But the filter selects on APPEARANCE: it keeps "Review of AI" and "Journal of Technology Law and Practice" (fake/wrong venues that look like journals) and drops the honest blogs. The filter is precisely inverted against this failure — it removes the entries whose metadata is harmless and retains the ones that are confidently false.

THREE FURTHER INDEPENDENT LEAKS:
(a) "that sentence's own [n]" is UNDEFINED for most FACT sentences: in report.md, 33 of 56 cited sentences carry MORE THAN ONE [n]. Picking one to name asserts a sole author for a claim fused from several sources — fabrication by selection.
(b) [n] points at the PAGE, not the STUDY. A large share of sources are secondary reporters (Hamilton Project, Cleveland Fed, Harvard Gazette, MIT Sloan Ideas Made to Matter, equitablegrowth.org) that DESCRIBE third-party work. Replacing "The study" with "the named referent of its own [n]" attributes a reported finding to the reporting page's metadata. This is Path #1 and #2, generalized.
(c) SELF-CONTRADICTORY CONTRACT + an unre-verified mutation. The contract states CLASS F permits only DELETIONS from an audited drop-list — "deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible." Then F+A "replace[s] every orphan subject… with the named referent," which MUTATES the subject NP of a byte-identical, span-entailed sentence and never re-runs `verify_sentence_provenance` / the entailment judge on the result. F+A silently reintroduces exactly the operation F exists to forbid. Separately, the fallback of harvesting authors "from the baskets" means byte-matching against raw scraped page-text blobs — which contain other papers' authors, editorial listings, and strings like "Microsoft Corporation > ∥ GitHub Inc." (num=79). A substring check against an entire scraped page is not a guard; almost any plausible name passes it.

Per SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md §5 — "FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE… Any design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores." This design leaks, demonstrably, on real artifacts. Unshippable as specified. (Note the strategic premise — that RACE strips [n] and the bibliography, so prose naming is the only source-quality channel — is CORRECT and worth pursuing; it is the implementation's root of trust that is rotten.)
input.fix:
The premise survives; the implementation must be rebuilt around SPAN-verified attribution, not bib-field byte-copying. Attribution may ship ONLY when it passes all four gates, in order:

1. SPAN-RESIDENT NAMES ONLY. The injected author/venue string must be a byte-substring of the ENTAILED SPAN ITSELF (`supporting_members[].direct_quote`), not of the bib record's metadata fields. Entry num=1 is the model case: its direct_quote literally contains "Daron Acemoglu and Pascual Restrepo 4 Journal of Economic Perspectives" — that attribution is span-grounded and safe. This restores the actual moat (span entailment) as the root of trust.
2. CROSSREF TITLE-MATCH GATE. A DOI may be used for backfill only if the Crossref record's title fuzzy-matches the bib entry's `source_title` (say, token-set ratio >= 0.9). This single check kills every path above: num=82 ("Research on AI and the labor market is still in the first inning" vs "The China Syndrome") and num=53 ("Technology Adoption and… Clerical Workers" vs "Inequality and City Size") both fail instantly. Run this as a standalone audit FIRST — it is free and tells you the true coverage.
3. SINGLE-CITATION GATE. Inject attribution only into sentences carrying exactly ONE [n] (23 of 56 in the current report). Never name a source for a multi-[n] fused claim.
4. PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE. Only attribute when the cited URL is the study's own venue/preprint host (doi.org, aeaweb, nber, arxiv, mdpi, sciencedirect, publisher domains) — never a reporting page (news, gazette, blog, think-tank commentary, "ideas-made-to-matter"). Orphan-subject SUBSTITUTION must be dropped entirely: it mutates a CLASS F sentence. Attribution may only be PREPENDED as an appositive; if a mutation is ever required, the resulting sentence must be re-run through `verify_sentence_provenance` and dropped on any failure.

Expect this to collapse coverage to roughly 4-15 of 105 entries — which is the honest answer, and is why the claimed +0.015 to +0.020 is not supported: the mechanism ("author + venue + N per claim") can only fire on a small minority of facts. Before paying for any compose, run the audit in gate 2 and report the count of entries that survive all four gates. If that number is under ~20, the lever cannot buy the claimed comprehensiveness points and should be dropped in favor of a source-quality channel that does not depend on this metadata layer (e.g. naming only the handful of canonical studies whose author/venue strings sit inside their own verified spans).
